ksa_text: Knowledge of data classification, cataloging, and metadata management systems, including automated data discovery, business glossary integration, metadata standards, and the role of metadata in enabling governance and self-service consumption.

Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DA-K-014 (DA, Knowledge) maps to Quantitative Analyst scope as moderate fit. Composite score 0.4936 clears the moderate fit threshold of 0.43. Top framework anchors: nist_ai_rmf=0.494, eu_ai_act=0.455, dcwf=0.413. Seniority fit: appropriate; content reflects IC-to-senior-IC execution work within quantitative analysis, statistical modeling, and financial analytics.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 include upheld. Score 0.4936 for moderate fit domain is consistent with ANL IC-level scope. No internal contradictions identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does KSA DA-K-014 (DA, composite 0.4936) appear in published data analyst / BI role profiles and workforce frameworks?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Survey of DCWF 421 and DDAT Data Architect profiles; DA competencies at awareness level appear in senior IC data analyst role profiles does not surface this DA-domain content as a core or common requirement for IC-to-senior-IC data analyst roles. Score 0.4936 may reflect incidental framework overlap. Without direct analytics-domain text confirmation, external ground truth does not support inclusion at this signal level.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 include overturned. Insufficient external evidence for DA domain content at composite 0.4936 without confirming text signals. Revised to exclude.

decision_rationale: Single adversarial overturn by P4 external ground truth. Final decision: exclude. Composite 0.4936, domain DA. Medium confidence — one agent challenged and overturned the preliminary assessment.
